title: Appeals Committee
event_details_description: The Burlington Appeals Committee is an administrative tribunal established by City Council in August 2025 (DGM-57-25).  The Appeals Committee hears appeals from decisions issued by the City related to it’s Licensing By-laws.  It also hears appeals from orders issued by the City related to it’s Property Standards By-law. The Appeals Committee makes final decisions that are not reviewed by City Council.

title: Pipeline to Permit Committee
event_details_description:
Standing committee and City Council meetings are held at City Hall, 426 Brant St. Written delegations can be submitted by email to Legislative Services at
clerks@burlington.ca
by noon the business day before the meeting date. Correspondence will be circulated to committee members.

title: Audit Committee
event_details_description:
Standing committee and City Council meetings are held at city hall, 426 Brant St. Requests to speak at this meeting can be made by completing the online delegation registration form at
www.burlington.ca/delegate
, by submitting a written request by email to Legislative Services at
clerks@burlington.ca
or by phoning 905-335-7777, ext. 7481 by noon the business day before the meeting date.
If you do not wish to speak, but would like to submit your comments in writing, please email your comments to
clerks@burlington.ca
. Written correspondence will be circulated to committee members.

event_details_description:

, by submitting a written request by email to Legislative Services at

or by phoning 905-335-7777, ext. 7481 by noon the Friday before the meeting date. If you do not wish to speak, but would like to submit your comments in writing, please email your comments to

. Written correspondence will be circulated to committee members.

title: Appeals Committee
event_details_description: The Burlington Appeals Committee is an administrative tribunal established by City Council in August 2025 (DGM-57-25).  The Appeals Committee hears appeals from decisions issued by the City related to it’s Licensing By-laws.  It also hears appeals from orders issued by the City related to it’s Property Standards By-law. The Appeals Committee makes final decisions that are not reviewed by City Council.

title: Heritage Burlington Advisory Committee
event_details_description:
The public is welcome to delegate to an advisory committee meeting or attend as an observer.  Participation can be remote or in-person. For information on how to speak at a meeting or to get information on how to attend remotely or in-person, email
clerks@burlington.ca
.
Heritage Burlington Advisory Committee is a committee made up of community volunteers and reports to City Council through the Committee of the Whole. The mandate of the committee is to provide advice to Council on issues related to the conservation of Burlington's cultural heritage and to promote community awareness and understanding of Burlington's cultural heritage. For any additional information, please see www.Burlington.ca/heritageburlington.
Please note Advisory Committees are not webcasted. There is no link to a video at HTML-Minutes and Video

title: Regular Meeting of Council
event_details_description:
City Council meetings are held at city hall, 426 Brant St. Requests to speak at this meeting can be made by completing the online delegation registration form at
www.burlington.ca/delegate
, by submitting a written request by email to Legislative Services at
clerks@burlington.ca
or by phoning 905-335-7777, ext. 7481 by noon the business day before the meeting date. If you do not wish to speak, but would like to submit your comments in writing, please email your comments to
clerks@burlington.ca
. Correspondence (delegation notes and written comments) will be circulated to committee members.
Council meetings are where the final approval takes place of items that went to the meetings of the Committee of the Whole, Pipeline to Permit Committee, Council Workshop and Audit Committee. Meeting agendas, dates or times may change. To verify information, contact the Office of the City Clerk by email at
clerks@burlington.ca
or at 905-335-7777 Ext. 7481. Note: Special meetings of Council may be scheduled as required.

title: Council Workshop Committee
event_details_description:
Council Workshop meetings are held at city hall, 426 Brant St. Workshops are live webcasted, and archived on the city website. Council Workshop meets when time is required for training, discussions with other levels of government and outside agencies, workshops on complex matters, and strategic planning. Delegations are not permitted at Council Workshops.
Meeting agendas, dates or times may change. To verify information, contact Legislative Services at
clerks@burlington.ca

title: Committee of Adjustment
event_details_description:
Committee of Adjustment
meetings will be held in-person at City Hall Council Chambers, 426 Brant St. and virtually via Zoom videoconferencing technology.
Requests to delegate at a meeting can be made by submitting a written request by email to the Secretary Treasurer of the Committee of Adjustment at committeeofadjustment@burlington.ca or by phoning 905-335-7777, ext. 7629 by noon the business day before the meeting is to be held. You are encouraged to pre-register as a delegate as soon as possible to facilitate an orderly registration process.
It is recommended that virtual delegates include their intended remarks, which will be circulated to all members in advance, as a backup to any disruptions in technology issues that may occur.
If you do not wish to delegate, but would like to submit correspondence, please email your comments to committeeofadjustment@burlington.ca. Any notes and comments will be circulated to the applicant, staff and members in advance of the meeting and will forming part of the public record.
The link will be active 30 minutes before the start time.
Applications will be heard in the order that they appear on the agenda or otherwise stated by the Chair.

title: Appeals Committee
event_details_description: The Burlington Appeals Committee is an administrative tribunal established by City Council in August 2025 (DGM-57-25).  The Appeals Committee hears appeals from decisions issued by the City related to it’s Licensing By-laws.  It also hears appeals from orders issued by the City related to it’s Property Standards By-law. title: Council Information Package
event_details_description:
Council Information Packages (CIPS) are published every week on Friday afternoons for members of City Council and the public. They can include information reports and memos from staff, inter-governmental correspondence from the provincial and federal government, other municipalities and from local boards and committees
Council Information Packages
published before January 2025 can be found on the
Council Information Package Page.
Members of City Council may request that an information item be placed on the next available Committee of the Whole agenda.
If you require the CIP in an alternative format or with communication supports, please submit an
Accessible Document and Communication request.
